Get Out (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $175,837,935Details
International Box Office $76,459,470
Worldwide Box Office $252,297,405
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,373,967
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,264,906
Total $14,638,873
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Now that Chris and his girlfriend, Rose, have reached the meet-the-parents milestone of dating, she invites him for a weekend getaway upstate with Missy and Dean. At first, Chris reads the family’s overly accommodating behavior as nervous attempts to deal with their daughter’s interracial relationship, but as the weekend progresses, a series of increasingly disturbing discoveries lead him to a truth that he could have never imagined.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$33,377,060 (19.0% of total gross)
Legs:5.27 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:69.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$5,000,000 (worldwide box office is 50.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,781 opening theaters/3,143 max. theaters, 7.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $234,443,329

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 24th, 2017 (Wide) by Universal
January 26th, 2018 (Limited) by Universal, released as Get Out (2017) (Re-Release)
International Releases: March 7th, 2017 (Wide), released as Pradink (Lithuania)
March 10th, 2017 (Wide) (Philippines)
March 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Trinidad)
March 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Greece)
March 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Singapore)
Video Release: May 9th, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for violence, bloody images, and language including sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2458 (Cert #50582), 1/11/2017)
Running Time: 104 minutes
Award nomination: 2018 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Actors Making Their Directorial Debut, Prologue
Plot point: Abusive Therapists, African Americans, Hit and Run, Hypnotism, Interracial Romance, Magical Technology, Missing Person, Photography, Relationships Gone Wrong, Unbelieving Police, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: Suburbs, Therapists / Therapy
Subgenre: Psychological Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Blumhouse, QC Entertainment, Universal Pictures, Monkeypaw Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Now that Chris and his girlfriend, Rose, have reached the meet-the-parents milestone of dating, she invites him for a weekend getaway upstate with Missy and Dean. At first, Chris reads the family’s overly accommodating behavior as nervous attempts to deal with their daughter’s interracial relationship, but as the weekend progresses, a series of increasingly disturbing discoveries lead him to a truth that he could have never imagined.
